title: Home Renovation Permits in Westchester NY: What You Actually Need to Know
excerpt: Every town in Westchester has its own permit process, and getting it right matters. Here's what homeowners need to know before starting a renovation.
content:
If you're planning a home renovation in Westchester County, you're going to hear the word "permits" a lot. And for good reason. Whether you're in Scarsdale, Mamaroneck, or Eastchester, every municipality has a building department that reviews and approves your project before you break ground.

This isn't bureaucracy for its own sake. Permits exist to make sure your renovation is safe, meets code, and protects your home's value. But the process can feel confusing if you don't know what to expect.

Why Your Town Has Its Own Building Department

Westchester County includes dozens of towns and villages, and each one runs its own building department. That's just how it works here. Rye has different inspectors than Harrison. New Rochelle has different timelines than Hastings-on-Hudson. Tuckahoe, Pelham, Bronxville—they all review projects independently.

This matters because it means there's no single Westchester permit process. You can't apply once and call it done. You work with your specific town's department.

What Actually Gets Permitted

Not every renovation needs a permit. A fresh coat of paint? No. New fixtures? Usually no. But structural work, electrical upgrades, plumbing changes, additions, and anything involving your home's footprint? Yes. Those all require permits.

If you're planning a home addition or second-story addition, you're definitely in permit territory. Same with kitchen remodels that move walls or add outlets, bathroom renovations that touch plumbing, or a primary suite addition. Anything that touches the bones of your house needs approval.

The Design-Build Advantage

Here's where working with the right architect and general contractor matters. When your designer and your builder are the same person—or work as one team—the permit process actually moves faster. There's no finger-pointing between two separate companies about who's responsible for what.

A licensed architect in New York who also holds a general contractor license can prepare permit drawings that actually work. They understand what the building department wants to see because they know both sides of the equation. They've submitted hundreds of these applications.

That's the advantage of a design-build firm. Your architect Westchester professional isn't just drawing something beautiful—they're designing something that will pass inspection in your specific town and get built efficiently.

Starting Your Project Right

Before you hire anyone, talk to your building department. Call them. They're surprisingly helpful. Ask what's required for your type of project. Ask what their timeline looks like.

Then hire a general contractor Westchester County who knows how to work in your town. Someone who's done home renovation work in Westchester before. Someone who's worked with your specific building department and knows their expectations.

This is where experience counts. A contractor who's built additions in Larchmont, Pelham Manor, and White Plains has seen different departments and different standards. They know how to adapt.

If you're ready to start a renovation and want to do it right, call House Architecture & Construction at (914) 224-7383 for a free consultation. We'll walk you through what your town requires and what the process actually looks like.
